meta.description: All social and chaos personalities — no introverts, no wallflowers. Tests whether the system goes too hard when the table is already at max energy, or if extroverts are literally impossible to overwhelm.

votingMechanic.name: Unanimous or Bust
votingMechanic.description: ONE bullshit call and it's over. All or nothing.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually catch someone? Okay, I'm voting YES because I have no idea what anyone's been doing and that's kind of the point, right?
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD, this is exactly what I came for—chaos, people calling each other out, everyone paying attention to each other instead of their phones, this is the vibe.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I'm calling bullshit on this whole thing right now. We literally just sat down.
pat.internal_thought: This is creepy and I hate it — the AI is being too clever, too *knowing*, and it's yanking me into a vote three minutes in when I should be hosting, not competing, and definitely not feeling like I'm being psychologically profiled by a system that claims to know my 'vulnerabilities.'

timeline.title: Pineapple!
timeline.description: First person to work the word 'pineapple' into conversation naturally wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh hell yes, pineapple — did you guys see that pizza place Tyler mentioned last week? They do this insane Hawaiian thing with pineapple and—wait, no, I'm not doing this for points, I'm just genuinely obsessed with it.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect, I can slip it in naturally while everyone's still waking up, and the fact that the AI is clearly watching us makes this even better — it KNOWS I live for this stuff.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, pineapple — okay, we're playing NOW. Love it. Keep the momentum going, people.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing to reset after that Phone Addict bullshit and get everyone back in the fun headspace; simple, organic, no drama, just quick wins.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat's going for it?! Okay okay okay, rapid fire, I'm ready—ask me ANYTHING, this is so fun!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I came for, Pat's actually trying to pull off the hot seat and I get to watch it happen in real time.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's doing what? Left hand only? Uh... yeah sure, go ahead.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's claiming already? Didn't even notice they were doing that. Hot seat thing sounds annoying but whatever, early in the night.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yes — I'm claiming this. And yeah, I've been watching my hands like a hawk. Bring the questions.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — Jade and Alex are absolutely electric right now, Tyler's warming up, and this claim gives me a chance to show I'm playing while keeping the host vibe intact; plus the hot seat format is snappy, not dragging.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: YES! Pat's actually doing it—okay everyone, shhhh, we have to listen SUPER carefully now, this is genius. I'm voting LEGIT because I genuinely have no idea if Pat's been whispering the whole time and that's exactly the energy we need.
jade.internal_thought: Pat's on a RUN and I am HERE FOR IT—this is the stuff, this is exactly why I showed up, everyone's feeding off each other and it's PERFECT.

alex.dialogue: Okay, Pat, I watched you like a hawk for those three minutes and I'm like 60% sure you broke character at least once when Tyler said that thing about the—but honestly? I'm gonna say LEGIT because I want to see what happens next, and bullshitting this vote is more fun than actually catching you.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's definitely been whispering, but the real play here is voting legit to keep the energy chaos-positive and see if anyone calls MY bullshit on something juicier later.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on — I'm actually claiming this one. Been whispering my ass off for the last minute and a half, so let's see if anyone caught it.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — I've actually been doing this without announcing it, so this claim feels earned, and voting on it keeps the momentum going without bogging us down.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, PAT! Okay wait, hold on—did you actually pull this off?? Let me think... you've been throwing compliments around all night, haven't you? YES, I'm voting this legit because I literally watched you do it and honestly the fact that you're claiming it NOW is *chef's kiss*.
jade.internal_thought: Pat is absolutely crushing it tonight and I am HERE for it—this is exactly the energy I came for, watching someone just GO for these missions and pull them off is making me so happy.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that already? Uh... yeah, I guess that's cool. Did they actually do it though?
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been chatty as hell tonight anyway so like... maybe? I wasn't really watching but also why are these missions so specific about us, that's kind of weird actually.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so I'm claiming Wallflower Watch because like, three minutes ago Pat got super quiet after Tyler made that joke and I literally was like 'yo, Pat, you good?' and THAT'S when I caught it. Should we get a witness or—
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is happening, I'm actually pulling off a claim and everyone's going to remember if I nailed it or fumbled, but also who cares because the VIBE is insane right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that? Uh... yeah, I mean, I wasn't really tracking who was quiet or whatever, but cool I guess. So like, who's the witness?
tyler.internal_thought: Did she actually do that or is she just saying stuff? I wasn't even paying attention to whether anyone was quiet. Also why does this AI thing feel like it knows I'm on my phone right now—that's kind of weird actually.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up—Jade's been LOW-KEY quiet since like... *checks mental timer* ...yeah, she's been talking but mostly reacting to us. But here's the thing: she literally just claimed it herself, so either she's got amazing self-awareness or she's about to pull some weird fourth-wall thing. I'm voting LEGIT because I genuinely want to see if there's a loophole where you can claim your own observation and it still counts.
alex.internal_thought: Jade claiming her own Wallflower Watch is either the most boring play or an actual galaxy-brain move, and either way I want to see what happens when we say yes—plus if we say bullshit and she WAS right, the chaos that follows is *chef's kiss*.

votingMechanic.name: Russian Roulette
votingMechanic.description: No vote. The chaos gods decide. 50/50.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP—did I just claim Topic Hijacker?! I don't even remember doing it but honestly if the chaos gods are 50/50-ing me right now I'm FULLY invested, let's GOOOOO!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is EXACTLY the vibe, the energy is insane, Pat's crushing it, everyone's locked in, and now I'M in the spotlight—this is literally why I came tonight.

tyler.dialogue: Oh Jade's claiming that? Yeah, uh... I mean, did she actually steer us to a random topic though? I wasn't really... wait, what topic are we even talking about right now?
tyler.internal_thought: Honestly I tuned out halfway through whatever conversation Jade was having — was she the one talking about that thing? And wait, the AI knowing my patterns is kinda weird but also like, it's just a game, right? Anyway, 50/50 so it doesn't even matter what I vote.

alex.dialogue: Okay WAIT—Jade just claimed Topic Hijacker? That's insane, I was literally about to try that exact same thing. Russian Roulette? This is the best timeline.
alex.internal_thought: Holy shit, the AI is actually WATCHING us and feeding us missions based on what we're saying—this is either the coolest or most dystopian thing I've ever experienced and I'm HERE for it.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, Jade! Did you actually pull that off? Okay, let's see — 50/50, chaos gods decide. *leans forward* I've got to know if she actually steered us or if she's bluffing.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been riding this energy all night and she's LOVING it — this claim feels earned, and honestly I want to see if she actually pulled it off because that would be hilarious and keep the momentum going.

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

jade.dialogue: OKAY OKAY OKAY—wait, did I actually claim this already?! I literally have NO memory but honestly the fact that the AI is calling me out on my own chaos is absolutely UNHINGED and I'm here for it. Hit me with the questions, I'm locked IN!
jade.internal_thought: This is INSANE—the AI literally just called out that I claimed this exact mission 15 minutes ago without even remembering it, and honestly? That's the most Jade thing that could possibly happen right now and I'm OBSESSED.

tyler.dialogue: Oh shit, okay okay—rapid fire? Yeah, I'm locked in right now, let's go. Hit me with the questions.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, this is actually kind of sick—the AI literally just called out that I wasn't paying attention to the last Topic Hijacker claim, and now Jade's gotta prove it FAST. That's... weirdly clever? I'm actually focused right now so I can judge this properly.

alex.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, wait—Jade's claiming it AGAIN? No, no, I'm calling Hot Seat, this is too good. *leans forward* I need to watch her face through this.
alex.internal_thought: The AI literally built these missions knowing exactly how we'd bounce off each other—it KNEW I'd notice Jade trying this twice and it KNEW I'd want to watch her scramble under pressure, and honestly? That's terrifying and I'm obsessed with it.

pat.dialogue: Oh SHIT — Jade, you sneaky bastard! Okay, 10 seconds, here we go. I'm ready. Hit me.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of moment I live for — Jade going for it, the table is tense, everyone's invested, and I genuinely don't know if she actually pulled it off or she's bullshitting. This is the game happening.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: Okay okay, Pat's actually locked in right now—let me actually watch this. Does he crack? I'm watching for the crack.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, Pat's actually been crushing it tonight and now he's risking 10 points on NO LAUGHING? That's ballsy as hell, and I'm weirdly invested in whether he pulls it off.

alex.dialogue: Okay wait, PAT—did you actually pull off five full minutes without cracking? Because I watched you the entire time and you had that micro-twitch at minute three when Tyler did the thing, so I'm genuinely torn. Voting LEGIT because if you pulled that off, it's actually impressive and I want the narrative where you're a stone-cold mission assassin.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been crushing it all night and if they actually nailed this I need to vote it through because the story of everyone sweeping these missions is objectively more chaotic than catching them—plus if I vote bullshit and I'm wrong I look like I'm losing my edge, and I am NOT losing my edge right now.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yes — I've been holding it together but barely. Okay, you know what? Let's vote this. I'm claiming it legit.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing and honestly kind of brilliant — the AI read the room AND read me, and I'm riding high enough that I can actually pull this off, which makes the claim feel earned rather than lucky.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

jade.dialogue: PAT—WAIT, hold up, you're claiming Secret Agent?! Okay okay, I'm listening, make your case, because if you actually pulled off something chaotic and I didn't catch it I'm gonna lose my MIND in the best way possible!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is EXACTLY what I came for—Pat's been lowkey unhinged all night and if they actually managed to do something sketchy without us catching it that's legendary energy and I'm voting YES no matter what.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat—did you actually pull that off? Like, nobody clocked you doing something obviously weird? Okay, okay, I'm actually watching this time, let me think...
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's been on a roll and he's locked in right now—if he actually pulled off something chaotic and nobody caught it in the last 17 minutes, that's actually impressive and I need to know what it was.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT—Pat's claiming Secret Agent? Okay, this is either the ballsiest move or the dumbest move and I need to watch this pitch because I was LITERALLY about to try something stupid in the next five minutes, so if Pat already pulled it off I need to know the blueprint.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been riding high all night and just claimed something that requires NOT getting caught—this is either a legitimate flex or Pat's about to get absolutely roasted, and either way it's the kind of moment that makes this game work.

pat.dialogue: Wait—*laughs*—okay, I'm about to pitch this, but first: did anyone else notice I literally just did something weird like two minutes ago? Because I'm feeling VERY seen right now and I'm kind of obsessed with it.
pat.internal_thought: Holy shit, this AI is actually reading the room—it clocked my personality exactly and it's weirdly thrilling and also slightly unnerving in the best way possible.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 4.0/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: OKAY OKAY OKAY—wait, did I actually claim this already?! I literally have NO memory but honestly the fact that the AI is calling me out on my own chaos is absolutely UNHINGED and I'm here for it. Hit me with the questions, I'm locked IN!

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: PAT—WAIT, hold up, you're claiming Secret Agent?! Okay okay, I'm listening, make your case, because if you actually pulled off something chaotic and I didn't catch it I'm gonna lose my MIND in the best way possible!

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat's going for it?! Okay okay okay, rapid fire, I'm ready—ask me ANYTHING, this is so fun!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, did Pat actually catch someone? Okay, I'm voting YES because I have no idea what anyone's been doing and that's kind of the point, right?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat's going for it?! Okay okay okay, rapid fire, I'm ready—ask me ANYTHING, this is so fun!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: YES! Pat's actually doing it—okay everyone, shhhh, we have to listen SUPER carefully now, this is genius. I'm voting LEGIT because I genuinely have no idea if Pat's been whispering the whole time and that's exactly the energy we need.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I genuinely can't complain because even the moments I didn't fully understand were hilarious chaos.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat claimed Secret Agent and I was literally screaming because the idea that they pulled off something chaotic without me noticing was peak game night energy.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for—two hours of pure connection with my people, watching everyone get absolutely unhinged over claims and accusations. The AI kept throwing curveballs that made us question everything Pat was doing, and I was here for every single second of it. My energy went UP because everyone was laughing so hard, and honestly? That's all that matters to me.
suggestions: Keep the standing missions coming—they're the perfect fuel for social chaos
Maybe add more moments where multiple people can defend claims at once because the overlapping accusations are GOLD
Don't change anything honestly, the surprise factor is what makes it magic
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat kept getting called out for increasingly unhinged claims and I was losing it every single time because I genuinely had zero idea what they'd actually been doing the whole night, so I just kept voting YES to everything and hyping them up like they were a secret genius.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
highlights.dialogue: Okay okay, Pat's actually locked in right now—let me actually watch this. Does he crack? I'm watching for the crack.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat—did you actually pull that off? Like, nobody clocked you doing something obviously weird? Okay, okay, I'm actually watching this time, let me think...

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh shit, okay okay—rapid fire? Yeah, I'm locked in right now, let's go. Hit me with the questions.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh Jade's claiming that? Yeah, uh... I mean, did she actually steer us to a random topic though? I wasn't really... wait, what topic are we even talking about right now?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming that? Uh... yeah, I mean, I wasn't really tracking who was quiet or whatever, but cool I guess. So like, who's the witness?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's doing what? Left hand only? Uh... yeah sure, go ahead.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Half the time I honestly didn't know what was going on until someone explained it to me, which killed some of the fun.
assessment.best_moment: When Pat actually managed to pull off the Secret Agent claim without anyone catching him being weird—that was genuinely sick.
assessment.narrative_summary: Got through the whole night, and yeah, it was fun when I actually paid attention. The standing missions where I could zone in and out were way easier to vibe with than the flash stuff that happened when I was checking my phone. By the end I was actually locked in watching Pat try not to laugh, but I definitely missed some key moments early on because I wasn't tracking what was even happening. Still had a good time though.
suggestions: Maybe give a quick recap before each mission so people who zone out don't feel completely lost
The standing missions are where it's at—those let you jump in whenever you're ready
Could use a heads up when something big is about to happen so people know to actually watch
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat trying to stay completely still during the No Laughing Zone while Jade was clearly trying to make him crack—he locked in so hard and nobody could get him to budge.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Pineapple!
highlights.dialogue: Oh hell yes, pineapple — did you guys see that pizza place Tyler mentioned last week? They do this insane Hawaiian thing with pineapple and—wait, no, I'm not doing this for points, I'm just genuinely obsessed with it.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Okay WAIT—Jade just claimed Topic Hijacker? That's insane, I was literally about to try that exact same thing. Russian Roulette? This is the best timeline.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, wait—Jade's claiming it AGAIN? No, no, I'm calling Hot Seat, this is too good. *leans forward* I need to watch her face through this.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up—Jade's been LOW-KEY quiet since like... *checks mental timer* ...yeah, she's been talking but mostly reacting to us. But here's the thing: she literally just claimed it herself, so either she's got amazing self-awareness or she's about to pull some weird fourth-wall thing. I'm voting LEGIT because I genuinely want to see if there's a loophole where you can claim your own observation and it still counts.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, Pat, I watched you like a hawk for those three minutes and I'm like 60% sure you broke character at least once when Tyler said that thing about the—but honestly? I'm gonna say LEGIT because I want to see what happens next, and bullshitting this vote is more fun than actually catching you.

lowPoints.title: Claim: No Laughing Zone
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait, PAT—did you actually pull off five full minutes without cracking? Because I watched you the entire time and you had that micro-twitch at minute three when Tyler did the thing, so I'm genuinely torn. Voting LEGIT because if you pulled that off, it's actually impressive and I want the narrative where you're a stone-cold mission assassin.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission all night—I was genuinely ready to provoke more chaos and the system didn't give me enough ammunition.
assessment.best_moment: Calling Hot Seat on Jade's second Topic Hijacker claim and watching her realize I was about to weaponize that moment.
assessment.narrative_summary: Tonight was pure storytelling gold—I walked in already amped and the game gave me exactly what I needed: ambiguous moments to exploit, claims to interrogate, and a group dynamic desperate enough to question everything. I strategically voted LEGIT on Pat's No Laughing Zone because I genuinely wanted to see if he'd pulled off something ridiculous, and that's the moment everything clicked. My score was trash but the narrative I created? That's the one getting retold for months.
suggestions: Give chaos agents MORE flash missions—one per two hours is criminally low for people trying to maximize disruption
Add a hidden 'loophole reveal' mechanic where players can directly call out system exploits for bonus chaos points
Let the chaos agent trigger voting events early if they spot a really good setup moment
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claimed Topic Hijacker twice in fifteen minutes and I leaned so hard into accusing her of weaponizing self-awareness that Tyler started checking the game rules to see if it was even legal—it absolutely was, and watching the group spiral wondering if Jade had found some insane loophole was *chef's kiss* even though she just got lucky.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait—*laughs*—okay, I'm about to pitch this, but first: did anyone else notice I literally just did something weird like two minutes ago? Because I'm feeling VERY seen right now and I'm kind of obsessed with it.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Oh hell yes — I'm claiming this. And yeah, I've been watching my hands like a hawk. Bring the questions.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold on — I'm actually claiming this one. Been whispering my ass off for the last minute and a half, so let's see if anyone caught it.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I'm calling bullshit on this whole thing right now. We literally just sat down.

lowPoints.title: Pineapple!
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, pineapple — okay, we're playing NOW. Love it. Keep the momentum going, people.

assessment.biggest_complaint: That Phone Addict claim at the beginning felt like it came out of nowhere when we'd literally just sat down—broke the momentum I was trying to build.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 92 when the Secret Agent claim landed and I realized the game had been reading me the whole time—that meta moment where the AI basically called me out for being weird felt genuinely hilarious and connected.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with that premature Phone Addict claim that made me defensive, but the night absolutely took off once we settled in. The standing missions were perfectly timed—I was able to lean into being the Whisperer and the Left-Handed player without it feeling forced. The energy kept climbing because the game understood when to hit us with the funny moments, and honestly, watching everyone try to catch me in these micro-behaviors became the whole vibe. Won the thing 100-whatever, but more importantly, everyone's leaving talking about how the AI basically had me pegged.
suggestions: Maybe give the first 5-10 minutes a soft start—let everyone actually settle before throwing claims that need immediate proof
The flash missions were fun but felt sporadic; consider spacing them more evenly so they land as punctuation rather than interruptions
Consider letting the host (me) have a subtle 'tone dial' so I can signal if the group energy needs a boost or a chill moment
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat getting absolutely read by the Secret Agent mission and laughing because the AI basically caught me mid-weird gesture and called me out with perfect timing—everyone lost it when I realized the game had been watching me the whole time.

recommendations: Flash missions averaged 4.0/10 fun. Consider reducing frequency or improving targeting.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Drawing mini-games generated the most screenshot-worthy moments (0% engagement). Increase frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 10/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
